comparison_target: Cass, Remnic, and TencentDB Agent Memory virtual-best capability envelope

scoring_policy.rule: Each row uses the strongest verified upstream capability. One hard blocker cannot be cancelled by wins elsewhere.
scoring_policy.narrowed_claim: Agent Recipes is stronger for safe, review-gated, executable experience reuse.
scoring_policy.overall_claim: Agent Recipes is stronger across every memory, compression, integration, recovery, governance, and execution capability.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic procedural mining plus Cass reflection plus TencentDB layered extraction
dimensions.reason: Agent Recipes handles corrections, courses, artifacts, and run evidence through cards and review-gated patch drafts; the fixed 19-project cohort passed, but arbitrary-domain completeness is not proven.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic review queue; Cass candidate maturity; TencentDB automatic memory writes
dimensions.reason: Candidate cards and patch drafts cannot become formal recipes without explicit review accept.
Strict lookup can refuse weak matches and is coupled to exact active recipe selection and lock creation.
Execution binds recipe id, version, hash, claim limits, and finite expiry before high-risk work.

dimensions.virtual_best: Cass richer feedback and maturity plus Remnic trajectory statistics
dimensions.reason: Agent Recipes now has 15 cause-specific kinds, exact lock/version/hash attribution, and a tested boundary that prevents execution or dependency failures from punishing recipes. Cass retains broader session-level and implicit feedback. The narrowed contract remains parity until both run one fixed feedback benchmark.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic snapshots, revert, tombstone, revocation, and consolidation undo
dimensions.reason: Agent Recipes matches the narrowed recipe need for tombstone, revocation, anti-resurrection, retirement, and active-version selection; it does not match Remnic's broader memory snapshot system.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic provenance and TencentDB layered source references
dimensions.reason: Source trace, cannot-claim, quarantine, evidence packs, and formal-recipe boundaries are machine-readable and enforced.

dimensions.virtual_best: No same-task cross-project benchmark is currently available
dimensions.reason: After the failed V5 cohort and the review-gated PIP repair, the predefined unseen V8 gate passed with 5 recipe wins, 1 tie, 0 baseline wins, 0 wrong locks, and a verified no-match. This is a local narrow production-effect result, not universal task superiority.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic multi-index recall plus TencentDB layered recall
dimensions.reason: The fixed 24-recipe SampleProject cohort retained 227 source rows but scored 111 unique expectations. Core passed 55/55 positives with zero false recalls; Cognee and Graphiti projections passed 53/55 with 3.6% false recall; live local Qwen passed 47/55 with 6.3% false recall and about 31 ms mean query latency. All 15 pure no-match cases were rejected. This closes the fixed local comparison gap, not native Graphiti/Cognee or arbitrary-domain quality.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic hosted CI, releases, history, migrations, and broad client integrations
dimensions.reason: Local engineering gates pass: supervised Qwen recovery, real 0.1.0 to 0.1.1 upgrade/rollback with unchanged event bytes, clean wheel installs on Python 3.11/3.13/3.14, and full 261-test runs on 3.11 and 3.13. Public repository history, release v0.1.1, and hosted CI run 29115605471 on Python 3.11/3.12/3.13 now pass. The blocker remains only because the exact release has no second physical-machine reproduction.

dimensions.virtual_best: Cass and TencentDB are smaller than Remnic but still require larger runtimes or external components
dimensions.reason: The minimal core has zero runtime dependencies and zero required external services. The public 0.1.1 wheel is 210096 bytes and includes the optional Qwen service command.
Agent Recipes intentionally does not rebuild a general conversation-memory platform in its core.
TencentDB has a real layered offload system; Agent Recipes has no equivalent and does not need one in the narrowed core.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic plus TencentDB OpenClaw and Hermes integration
dimensions.reason: Local Codex MCP smoke passes, but broad production host integration is not proven.

dimensions.virtual_best: TencentDB checkpoints, retries, backup recovery, and degradation
dimensions.reason: Agent Recipes has idempotent events and fail-closed writes, but no equivalent long-running learning scheduler; it will only add one after real interruption evidence.

dimensions.virtual_best: Remnic and TencentDB are MIT; Cass is restricted for this agent context
dimensions.reason: Cass remains audit-only and cannot be downloaded, executed, benchmarked, copied, or integrated under the current conservative boundary.

current_verdict.allowed_claim: Agent Recipes has a local narrow lead in governance and execution safety; fixed fresh-agent and recall gates pass; local 0.1.1 service recovery, upgrade/rollback, and multi-Python distribution gates pass. Hosted CI and second-machine evidence still block the narrowed superiority claim.
forbidden_claims: Agent Recipes has surpassed Cass, Remnic, and TencentDB Agent Memory combined.
The 6/9 internal gate count is a three-repository superiority score.
One passing V8 cohort proves universal fresh-agent or rendered production quality.
Three Python environments on one Mac equal a second physical-machine reproduction.
